rationale: RocksDB uses background threads (prefixed 'rocksdb:') for compaction and memtable flushing. These are CPU-intensive, long-running operations that compete with foreground read/write threads for CPU time. Under CPU oversubscription, background compaction threads can preempt latency-critical query threads, causing tail latency spikes. Deprioritizing 'rocksdb:' threads lets the kernel schedule foreground query threads first, reducing P99.9 latency without starving compaction (which tolerates delay).

threads.purpose: Low-priority background compaction threads. Perform LSM-tree compaction to merge and sort SSTables. CPU-intensive and long-running.
High-priority background threads for memtable flush operations. Flush in-memory writes to disk as SSTables. Less CPU-intensive than compaction but still background work.
